title: Check every syscall return and handle errno
description: Treat each syscall as fallible: inspect the return, branch on errno, and retry EINTR and short writes correctly.
body: Use when calling read/write/open/connect or any syscall wrapper. Do not ignore the return value or assume a write wrote everything, because syscalls fail (EINTR, EAGAIN, ENOSPC) and write may transfer fewer bytes than requested. Actions: check the return, branch on errno for the specific condition, retry on EINTR, loop on partial read/write until complete or error, and surface ENOSPC/EACCES as real failures. Validation signals include tests that inject short writes and EINTR and confirm the code retries/recovers rather than silently truncating. Risk boundary: an unchecked syscall on a data path can silently corrupt or truncate; treat ignored errno on durability or network writes as a blocking defect.
